title: ForgeConfig
description:
Top-level Graff configuration merged from all sources (defaults, file,
environment).

retry.description: Retry settings applied at the system level to all IO operations.

max_search_lines.description: Maximum number of lines returned by a single file search operation.

max_search_result_bytes.description: Maximum number of bytes returned by a single file search operation.

max_fetch_chars.description: Maximum number of characters returned from a URL fetch.

max_stdout_prefix_lines.description:
Maximum number of lines captured from the leading portion of shell
command output.

max_stdout_suffix_lines.description:
Maximum number of lines captured from the trailing portion of shell
command output.

max_stdout_line_chars.description: Maximum number of characters per line in shell command output.

max_line_chars.description: Maximum number of characters per line when reading a file.

max_read_lines.description: Maximum number of lines read from a file in a single operation.

max_file_read_batch_size.description: Maximum number of files read in a single batch operation.

http.description: HTTP client settings including proxy, TLS, and timeout configuration.

max_file_size_bytes.description: Maximum file size in bytes permitted for read operations.

max_image_size_bytes.description: Maximum image file size in bytes permitted for read operations.

tool_timeout_secs.description:
Maximum time in seconds a single tool call may run before being
cancelled.

auto_open_dump.description:
Whether to automatically open HTML dump files in the browser after
creation.

debug_requests.description: Directory where debug request files are written; disabled when absent.

custom_history_path.description:
Path to the conversation history file; defaults to the global history
location when absent.

max_conversations.description: Maximum number of conversations shown in the conversation list.

max_sem_search_results.description:
Maximum number of candidate results returned from the initial semantic
search vector query.

sem_search_top_k.description: Number of top results retained after re-ranking in semantic search.

services_url.description:
Base URL of the Graff services API used for semantic search and
indexing.

max_extensions.description: Maximum number of file extensions included in the agent system prompt.

auto_dump.description:
Format used when automatically creating a session dump after task
completion; disabled when absent.

max_parallel_file_reads.description: Maximum number of files read concurrently during batch operations.

model_cache_ttl_secs.description: Time-to-live in seconds for the cached model API list.

session.description:
Default model and provider configuration used when not overridden by
individual agents.

commit.description: Model and provider configuration used for commit message generation.

use_forge_committer.description:
Whether `graff commit` should override `GIT_COMMITTER_NAME` and
`GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL` with the Graff identity. Defaults to `true` via
the embedded `.forge.toml` defaults.

max_commit_count.description:
Maximum number of recent commits included as context for commit message
generation.

suggest.description:
Model and provider configuration used for shell command suggestion
generation.

temperature.description:
Output randomness for all agents; lower values are deterministic, higher
values are creative (0.0–2.0).

top_p.description:
Nucleus sampling threshold for all agents; limits token selection to the
top cumulative probability mass (0.0–1.0).

top_k.description:
Top-k vocabulary cutoff for all agents; restricts sampling to the k
highest-probability tokens (1–1000).

max_tokens.description:
Maximum tokens the model may generate per response for all agents
(1–100,000).

max_tool_failure_per_turn.description:
Maximum tool failures per turn before the orchestrator forces
completion.

max_requests_per_turn.description: Maximum number of requests that can be made in a single turn.

compact.description:
Context compaction settings applied to all agents; falls back to each
agent's individual setting when absent.

restricted.description:
Whether restricted mode is active; when enabled, tool execution requires
explicit permission grants.

tool_supported.description:
Whether tool use is supported in the current environment; when false,
all tool calls are disabled.

reasoning.description:
Reasoning configuration applied to all agents; controls effort level,
token budget, and visibility of the model's thinking process.

fast_mode.description:
When `true`, requests sent to OpenAI-series models (gpt-* / codex-*)
include `service_tier: "priority"` for the OpenAI Priority Processing
lane (Codex-style fast mode). Other providers/models silently ignore
this flag. Toggled at runtime via the `/fast` slash command.

providers.description:
Additional provider definitions merged with the built-in provider list.

Entries with an `id` matching a built-in provider override its fields;
entries with a new `id` are appended and become available for model
selection.

currency_symbol.description:
Currency symbol displayed in the shell rprompt next to the session cost
(e.g. `"$"`, `"€"`, `"₹"`). Defaults to `"$"`.

currency_conversion_rate.description:
Conversion rate applied to costs before display in the shell rprompt.
The raw USD cost is multiplied by this value, allowing costs to be shown
in a local currency. Defaults to `1.0` (no conversion).

verify_todos.description:
Enables the pending todos hook that checks for incomplete todo items
when a task ends and reminds the LLM about them.

max_end_hook_rearms.description:
Maximum number of times an `End`-lifecycle hook may re-arm the
orchestrator loop in a single run by injecting a follow-up message
(e.g. the pending-todos reminder).

Once the cap is reached, the orchestrator force-yields with an
`EndHookRearmLimitReached` interrupt rather than continuing to ping
the model. This bounds the "reminder + reword + reminder" loop that
otherwise only terminated at `max_requests_per_turn`.

Also used as the per-fingerprint cap in the pending-todos handler so
the same set of incomplete todos can't trigger more reminders than
the orchestrator is willing to honor anyway.

research_subagent.description:
Whether the deep research agent is available.

When set to `true`, the Sage agent is added to the agent list and
the `:sage` app command is enabled. Defaults to `false`.

subagents.description:
Enables subagent support via the task tool; when true the implementation
agent gains access to the `task` tool for delegating work to specialised
sub-agents, and the `sage` research-only agent tool is removed.
When false the `task` tool is disabled and `sage` is available instead.

openai_responses_websocket.description:
Sends OpenAI Responses API requests over a persistent WebSocket
connection (with `previous_response_id` continuation + warm-socket
reuse) for the `openai`, `openai-responses-compatible`, and `codex`
providers. Reduces per-turn latency on tool-heavy workflows. Defaults
to `true`; set to `false` to force the legacy HTTP path.

The `GRAFF_OPENAI_RESPONSES_WEBSOCKET` env var (or the legacy
`FORGE_OPENAI_RESPONSES_WEBSOCKET`) can opt in at runtime even when
the config is `false` — useful for one-off testing.

generate_titles.description:
When false, the asynchronous conversation-title generator is disabled.
Title generation runs in parallel with the main agent turn and is
useful for browsing conversations later in interactive mode, but it
adds an extra LLM round trip per conversation. Disable it for
scripted / `--prompt` workflows where conversation titles aren't
needed. Defaults to `true` to preserve historical behavior.

initial_backoff_ms.description: Initial backoff delay in milliseconds for retry operations

min_delay_ms.description: Minimum delay in milliseconds between retry attempts

backoff_factor.description: Backoff multiplication factor for each retry attempt

status_codes.description: HTTP status codes that should trigger retries

max_delay_secs.description: Maximum delay between retries in seconds

suppress_errors.description: Whether to suppress retry error logging and events

min_tls_version.description: Minimum TLS protocol version to use

max_tls_version.description: Maximum TLS protocol version to use

adaptive_window.description: Adaptive window sizing for improved flow control

TlsVersion.description: TLS version enum for configuring TLS protocol versions.

AutoDumpFormat.description: The output format used when auto-dumping a conversation on task completion.

ModelConfig.description: Pairs a provider and model together for a specific operation.

provider_id.description: The provider to use for this operation.

model_id.description: The model to use for this operation.

frequency.description: How frequently Graff checks for updates: daily, weekly, always, or never

auto_update.description: Whether to automatically install updates without prompting

UpdateFrequency.description: Frequency at which Graff checks for updates

Compact.description: Configuration for automatic context compaction for all agents

retention_window.description:
Number of most recent messages to preserve during compaction.
These messages won't be considered for summarization. Works alongside
eviction_window - the more conservative limit (fewer messages to
compact) takes precedence.

eviction_window.description:
Maximum percentage of the context that can be summarized during
compaction. Valid values are between 0.0 and 1.0, where 0.0 means no
compaction and 1.0 allows summarizing all messages. Works alongside
retention_window - the more conservative limit (fewer messages to
compact) takes precedence.

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to keep after compaction

token_threshold.description:
Maximum number of tokens before triggering compaction. This acts as an
absolute cap and is combined with
`token_threshold_percentage` by taking the lower value.

token_threshold_percentage.description:
Maximum percentage of the model context window used to derive the token
threshold before triggering compaction. This is combined with
`token_threshold` by taking the lower value.

turn_threshold.description: Maximum number of conversation turns before triggering compaction

message_threshold.description: Maximum number of messages before triggering compaction

model.description:
Model ID to use for compaction, useful when compacting with a
cheaper/faster model. If not specified, the root level model will be
used.

on_turn_end.description: Whether to trigger compaction when the last message is from a user

ReasoningConfig.description:
Controls the reasoning behaviour of a model, including effort level, token
budget, and visibility of the thinking process.

effort.description:
Controls the effort level of the model's reasoning.
Supported by openrouter and the Graff provider.

max_tokens.description:
Controls how many tokens the model can spend thinking.
Should be greater than 1024 but less than the overall max_tokens.
Supported by openrouter, anthropic, and the Graff provider.

exclude.description:
When true, the model thinks deeply but the reasoning is hidden from the
caller. Supported by openrouter and the Graff provider.

enabled.description:
Enables reasoning at the "medium" effort level with no exclusions.
Supported by openrouter, anthropic, and the Graff provider.

oneOf.description: No reasoning; skips the thinking step entirely.
Medium reasoning effort; the default for most providers.
Maximum reasoning effort; only available on select Anthropic models.

ProviderEntry.description:
A single provider entry defined inline in the Graff config file.

Inline providers are merged with the built-in provider list; entries with
the same `id` override the corresponding built-in entry field-by-field,
while entries with a new `id` are appended to the list.

id.description: Unique provider identifier used in model paths (e.g. `"my_provider"`).

api_key_var.description: Environment variable holding the API key for this provider.

url.description:
URL template for chat completions; may contain `{{VAR}}` placeholders
that are substituted from the credential's url params.

models.description:
URL template for fetching the model list; may contain `{{VAR}}`
placeholders.

response_type.description: Wire protocol used by this provider.

url_param_vars.description:
Environment variables whose values are substituted into `{{VAR}}`
placeholders in the `url` and `models` templates.

custom_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request to this provider.

provider_type.description: Provider category; defaults to `llm` when omitted.

auth_methods.description:
Authentication methods supported by this provider; defaults to
`["api_key"]` when omitted.

ProviderResponseType.description: Wire protocol a provider uses for chat completions.

ProviderUrlParam.description:
A URL parameter variable for a provider, used to substitute template
variables in URL strings.

name.description: The environment variable name used as the template variable key.

options.description:
Optional preset values for this parameter shown as suggestions in the
UI.

oneOf.description: Context engine provider for code indexing and search.

ProviderAuthMethod.description:
Authentication method supported by a provider.

Only the simple (non-OAuth) methods are available here; providers that
require OAuth device or authorization-code flows must be configured via the
file-based `provider.json` override instead.
